rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MACPRIN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Appoint Independent Successor Trustee To Administer And Terminate The Macprin Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/23/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/26/2024) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 3) The name and address of Terry Thompson's personal representative must be provided by a verified allegation, and notice must be given. Please also provide a copy of letters issued to the personal representative. If there is no personal representative, each heir of Terry Thompson and each devisee and executor named in Terry Thomson's will must be provided by a verified allegation, and notice must be given to each of them. See Probate Code section 17203(c)(1). 4) The address of Almarie Steward, alternate successor co-trustee named in the trust in the event the petitioner is unable or unwilling to serve, must be provided by a verified allegation, and notice must be given, absent a signed declination to serve. 5) The name and address of a proposed successor trustee of the Survivor's Trust must be provided by a verified allegation. The proposed trustee's acceptance of the trust and consent to act must also be filed, together with the beneficiaries' majority vote under section 7.1(b) of the trust, if any. 6) The name and address of a proposed successor trustee of the Bypass Trust must be provided by a verified allegation. The proposed trustee's acceptance of the trust and consent to act must also be filed, together with the beneficiaries' majority vote under section 7.1(b) of the trust, if any. 7) Please provide a description and fair market value of each asset on hand of the Survivor's Trust, plus estimated annual gross income therefrom, absent a bond waiver signed by each beneficiary and required by section 15602(b). 8) Please provide a description and fair market value of each asset on hand of the Bypass Trust, plus estimated annual gross income therefrom, absent a bond waiver signed by each beneficiary and required by section 15602(b). 9) Please provide a copy of the settlement agreement reached in 2023 and referred to in paragraph 9 of the petition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YUN TUNG YU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/23/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (3) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received by the assigned examiner, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B., but the scanned pleading does not appear to have an accounting attached to it. (4) Paragraphs 15 and 18 seem to have conflicting amounts for the assets on hand. (5) The prayer does not show the amount of statutory fee to the attorney. (6) The proposed distribution amounts do not appear to be correct. Please show the math. (7) How did the administrator arrive at 3/40 for each of the niblings' shares? The children of a predeceased sibling should share equally in what would have been their parent's 1/5 interest Deadline is 10/14/24 for providing the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE IRVING B. SACKS REVOCABLE TRUST DTD OCTOBER 17, 2013
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions To Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/9/24 to be heard with the new petition. Although the trust appears to have some contradictory provisions, such as Article Two and the gifts made in Article Seven, notice was not given to the two children. Notice must be given directly to a person entitled to notice, not in care of another. If a party entitled to notice is represented by an attorney, notice must also be given to the attorney, but not only to the attorney. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b)].

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KAREN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition was e-filed on 8/8/24. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (2) Item 3g(2) of the amended petition still does not fully indicate how the petitioners have priority. (3) Item 6f of the petition (and the amended petition) was not the first box that applies if the petitioners are the decedent's siblings. (When siblings are the nearest heirs to survive, there is no need to go beyond the predeceased parents.) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/11/24. The courtesy copies should be (1) the proof of publication of notice of the amended petition and (2) the petitioners' verified SUPPLEMENT to their amended petition, indicating that item 3g(2)(c) should have been completed to indicate that they are the decedent's siblings and item 6b, not 6f, of the amended petition should have been checked. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Please refrain from providing UNFILED copies. The Notice of Hearing is NOT the correct form of notice at this initial phase of an estate proceeding. Furthermore, mailing notice on 8/6/24 when the amended petition was not e-filed until 8/8/24 would have made notice defective had notice been required, and 8/8/24 was not at least 15 days before this hearing. Finally, the Judicial Council form DE-121 should have clearly indicated that it was a Notice of Amended Petition to Administer Estate, which is how published notice of the amended petition should also be shown.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARTIN PASCAL BEGADON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate will be set for 9/8/25. The personal representative must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 9/8/25 if no petition has been filed. (Thank you, Counsel, for the courtesy copies.)
